title: Tigridia citrina
intro: The Tigridia citrina, also known as the yellow tiger flower or Mexican gold, is a plant species belonging to the Iridaceae family, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers with distinctive markings. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Tigridia citrina include alkaloids, glycosides, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Tigridia citrina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This family includes over 200 genera and more than 1,800 species of flowering plants. Within the Iridaceae family, Tigridia citrina belongs to the subfamily Iridoideae, which is a large and diverse group of plants. The subfamily Iridoideae is further divided into several tribes, with Tigridia citrina being a member of the Tigridieae tribe. Tigridia citrina is also classified as a genus in its own right, with several species recognized within the genus.
uses: Tigridia citrina is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America, for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal problems. In some regions, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The roots and flowers of Tigridia citrina are often used to make infusions and decoctions for medicinal purposes. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
